title: Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace (video game)
content:
{{Top|rwm|imp|title=''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace''}} {{Youmay|the [[Star Wars video games|video game]]|the [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|film]] or other uses of [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace (disambiguation)|''The Phantom Menace'']]}} {{VideoGame |image=[[File:Ep1_PC_front.jpg]] |title=''Star Wars'': Episode I
''The Phantom Menace'' |developer=*[[LucasArts|LucasArts Entertainment Company]] *[[Big Ape Productions]] |publisher=LucasArts Entertainment Company |release date=*[[May 18]], [[1999]] {{C|[[Wikipedia:Personal computer|PC]]}}{{WebCite|url=https://www.ign.com/articles/1999/05/18/star-wars-hits-the-shelves|text=Star Wars Hits The Shelves|author=IGN Staff|date=1999-05-18|work=[[Wikipedia:IGN|IGN]]|archivedate=20121121210047}} *[[September 15]], 1999 {{C|[[Wikipedia:PlayStation (console)|PlayStation]]}}{{LucasArts|url=static/pr/tpm_psx_press_release.htm|text=THE ''STAR WARS''® SAGA COMES ALIVE FOR PLAYSTATION SEPTEMBER 15 IN LUCASARTS' ''STAR WARS: EPISODE I THE PHANTOM MENACE''™}} *[[January 17]], [[2024]] {{C|PlayStation 4, PlayStation 5}}{{WebCite|url=https://store.playstation.com/en-us/concept/10004910|text=STAR WARS Episode I - The Phantom Menace|date=2024-01-17|work=[[Wikipedia:PlayStation Store|PlayStation Store]]|archivedate=20240118052124}} |closed= |upc=023272315184 |engine= |genre=Action/Adventure |modes= |ratings=T (Teen) |platforms=*PC *PlayStation *PlayStation 4 *PlayStation 5 |series= |basegame= |expansions= |director= |producer= |designer= |programmer= |artist= |writer= |composer= |timeline=[[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]]''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' |prev= |next= }} {{Quote|The Adventure Begins|Tagline|}} '''''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace''''' is the action-adventure [[Star Wars video games|video game]] adaption of the ''[[Star Wars]]'' [[prequel trilogy]] film [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|''Star Wars: The Phantom Menace'']]. It was published by the [[LucasArts]] Entertainment Company and developed by Lucasarts in conjunction with [[Big Ape Productions]]. The game was released for [[Wikipedia:Personal computer|PC]] on [[May 18]], [[1999]], and for [[Wikipedia:PlayStation (console)|PlayStation]] on [[September 15]], 1999. ==Opening crawl== The game has two opening crawls. The first is identical to that of the movie: {{OpeningCrawl |name=Episode I
THE PHANTOM MENACE |text=Turmoil has engulfed the Galactic Republic. The taxation of trade routes to outlying star systems is in dispute. Hoping to resolve the matter with a blockade of deadly battleships, the greedy Trade Federation has stopped all shipping to the small planet of Naboo. While the congress of the Republic endlessly debates this alarming chain of events, the Supreme Chancellor has secretly dispatched two Jedi Knights, the guardians of peace and justice in the galaxy, to settle the conflict....}} The second crawl appears after the Coruscant level, following Senator Palpatine's proposal to call for a vote of no confidence in Supreme Chancellor Valorum: {{OpeningCrawl |name= |text=Queen Amidala's pleas to the Republic Senate have been in vain. Although Supreme Chancellor Valorum has been removed from office, and Senator Palpatine is poised to replace him, the Senate can take no action against the Trade Federation. Frustrated, the Queen has decided to return to her planet. The Jedi Council, meanwhile, has denied Qui-Gon's request to train young Anakin. While the boy's future remains uncertain, he has joined the Jedi as they escort the Queen to Naboo. Having convinced the Gungans to aid her people, Queen Amidala and her loyal followers plan to infiltrate the Theed palace, where they hope to capture the Trade Federation Viceroy and bring an end to the conflict....}} ==Plot summary== Although the plot closely follows the movie, it either expands on some minor events that could have been cut from the movie, or shows them from a different character's point of view. For example, it follows Amidala's "adventures" on Coruscant during Anakin Skywalker's interview in the Jedi Temple—something that is not shown in the movie. There are also some apparent inconsistencies or contradictions to the storyline in order to generate a new narrative. These include [[Jar Jar Binks/Legends|Jar Jar Binks]] being imprisoned on his return to [[Otoh Gunga/Legends|Otoh Gunga]] and the player having to rescue him to continue the story. ===Trade Federation Ship=== [[Qui-Gon Jinn/Legends|Qui-Gon Jinn]] and [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] boarded the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation's]] [[Saak'ak/Legends|flagship]]. While they were waiting to meet [[Viceroy of the Trade Federation|Viceroy]] [[Nute Gunray/Legends|Nute Gunray]], the room started filling with [[Poison gas/Legends|poison gas]]. The two Jedi fought their way out, destroying [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droids]] along the way. At one point, Obi-Wan was separated from Qui-Gon on the way to the hangar. As the Trade Federation had destroyed the Jedi's [[Radiant VII/Legends|ambassadorial ship]], Obi-Wan sneaked on board one of the [[C-9979 landing craft/Legends|landing craft]] bound for [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]]. ===Swamps of Naboo=== After landing on Naboo, Obi-Wan navigated his way through the [[Lianorm Swamp/Legends|swamps]] and encountered Jar Jar Binks, who led him to Qui-Gon. He fought some battle droids and hostile creatures along the way. After reuniting with Qui-Gon, Jar Jar told them about Otoh Gunga and reluctantly brought them there to take shelter from the Trade Federation. ===Otoh Gunga=== In Otoh Gunga, Qui-Gon and Obi-Wan realized that Jar Jar had been banished for his clumsiness, and had been forbidden from returning—on pain of death. Jar Jar got arrested and thrown into prison to await execution. [[Rugor Nass/Legends|Boss Nass]] and the [[Gungan High Council/Legends|Gungan High Council]] agreed to provide the Jedi a [[Tribubble bongo/Legends|bongo]] for their journey to [[Theed/Legends|Theed]] through the planet's [[Naboo Abyss/Legends|underwater core]]. While Qui-Gon tried to convince the Council to spare Jar Jar, Obi-Wan went to rescue Jar Jar. He used a [[Mind trick/Legends|Force mind trick]] to make the prison guards release Jar Jar, and then they made their way to the [[Bongo Bay|bongo bay]] to meet Qui-Gon and leave together. They navigated the bongo through the core and narrowly escaped from attacks by an [[Opee sea killer/Legends|opee sea killer]] and a [[Sando aqua monster/Legends|sando aqua monster]]. ===Gardens of Theed=== Upon arriving outside Theed, the three of them were about to cross a bridge leading directly to the city gates when a Trade Federation [[Armored Assault Tank/Legends|AAT]] blocked their way and destroyed the bridge. Obi-Wan was separated from Qui-Gon and Jar Jar, and had to take a longer route through the [[Royal Gardens|Theed Royal Gardens]] to enter the city. Along the way, he encountered [[Royal Naboo Security Forces/Legends|Royal Naboo Security Forces]] and joined them in fighting the battle droids. Upon reaching his destination, he destroyed the AAT and used [[Force push]] to activate the lever that unlocks the gates. ===Escape from Theed=== Qui-Gon, Obi-Wan, and Jar Jar managed to meet up with [[Padmé Amidala/Legends|Queen Amidala]], who told them her plan to escape from Naboo and go to [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] to persuade the [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Galactic Senate]] to help them. On the way to the [[Theed Hangar/Legends|hangar]], an explosion caused Obi-Wan and the Queen to be separated from Qui-Gon and the rest. Obi-Wan had to escort the Queen safely to the hangar on his own. Along the way, he destroyed several battle droids and helped some Naboo soldiers and civilians. They reunited with everyone in the hangar, where they boarded a [[J-type 327 Nubian royal starship/Legends|Royal Naboo Starship]]. ===Mos Espa=== While breaking through the Trade Federation's blockade, the starship came under heavy fire, and its [[T-14 hyperdrive generator/Legends|T-14 hyperdrive generator]] sustained severe damage. They decided to land on [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]] to make repairs. Qui-Gon, Amidala, and Jar Jar headed to [[Mos Espa/Legends|Mos Espa]] to find replacement parts. Qui-Gon defeated some [[Tusken Raider/Legends|Tusken Raiders]] who ambushed them along the way. In Mos Espa, they learned from the locals that [[Watto/Legends|Watto]], a junk dealer, had the parts they needed. With help from [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]], Watto's boy slave, Qui-Gon met Watto and made a bet with him for the hyperdrive generator in the [[Boonta Eve Classic/Legends|Boonta Eve Podrace]], which was held on the following day. Qui-Gon helped Anakin find a [[mass coupler]] and a [[servo control system]] to fix his [[Anakin Skywalker's Podracer/Legends|podracer]]. Concurrently, he completed some side quests: finding Jar Jar (who had gone missing) in a bathhouse, fighting some street thugs, saving [[Vek Drow/Legends|Vek Drow]] from robbers, and rescuing [[Tomo]] from [[Neg|Captain Neg's]] hideout. ===Mos Espa Arena=== [[File:JabbaChamp.jpg|thumb|250px|[[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba]] as seen in the game, with his [[Interpreter (droid)|translator droid]], and the corpse of [[Jabba's champion|his champion]] in the pit below]]In the [[Mos Espa Grand Arena/Legends|Mos Espa Grand Arena]], before the podrace started, [[Ann Gella/Legends|Ann Gella]] lured Qui-Gon into a trap set by [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba the Hutt]], who forced the Jedi Master to fight [[Jabba's champion|his champion]]. Qui-Gon slew the creature and received 50 [[Peggat/Legends|peggats]] to use in his bet with Watto. Qui-Gon and Watto struck a deal: If Anakin won the podrace, Watto would free Anakin and give Qui-Gon the T-14 hyperdrive generator; if Anakin lost, Watto would get the Royal Naboo Starship; either way, Watto would keep the 50 peggats. Qui-Gon encountered [[Jym Lang]], a [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] hired by Jabba to assassinate the podracer [[Teemto Pagalies/Legends|Teemto]], and used a Force mind trick to stop him from killing Teemto. Just before the race started, a [[Lizard boy|thief]] stole an [[Plasma ignition capacitor|ignition capacitor]] from Anakin's podracer and fled. Qui-Gon tracked down the thief, defeated a [[Scavenger (Mos Espa)|scavenger]], and helped Anakin retrieve the ignition capacitor. Anakin won the podrace and earned his freedom. ===Encounter in the Desert=== The locals, including Anakin's mother [[Shmi Skywalker Lars/Legends|Shmi Skywalker]], came to see Qui-Gon, Anakin, and their companions off. While heading back to the Naboo Royal Starship, Qui-Gon destroyed three [[DRK-1 Dark Eye probe droid/Legends|probe droids]] that attacked them. Shortly after, he was attacked by [[Darth Maul/Legends|Darth Maul]], who used the Force to trigger a rockslide to block his way. After fending off Maul's attacks, Qui-Gon found a path through the rocks and moved on. As Qui-Gon was approaching the starship, Maul showed up again and tried to destroy the hyperdrive generator. Qui-Gon managed to hold off Maul long enough before boarding the starship just as it took off, leaving Maul behind. ===Coruscant=== On [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], after Qui-Gon, Obi-Wan, and Anakin left for the [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]], Queen Amidala and [[Quarsh Panaka/Legends|Captain Panaka]] followed their [[Senate Guard/Legends|armed escorts]] to the [[Senate Building/Legends|Senate Chambers]] to meet [[Senator/Legends|Senator]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]]. However, [[Coruscant mercenary|mercenaries]] and a [[hover cannon]] killed their escorts and destroyed their [[EasyRide passenger airspeeder/Legends|air taxi]], forcing them to find their own way to the Senate Chambers. After Panaka traded his [[Electrobinoculars/Legends|electrobinoculars]] for a pair of [[Coruscant City Tours]] tickets, Panaka and Amidala tried to take the [[tour transport]] to the Senate Chambers but the transport left without them when a hover cannon fired at it. After destroying the hover cannon, Panaka left the Queen alone for a while and went to activate some controls. The mercenaries used the opportunity to kidnap the Queen and brought her into the [[Restricted Area]]. Panaka fought the mercenaries and found his way into the Restricted Area, where he rescued the Queen and defeated the mercenaries' [[Sun Guard]] [[Thug leader|leader]] who tried to stop them. They successfully reached the Senate Chambers and spoke to Senator Palpatine. ===Assault on Theed=== Disappointed by the politicking and corruption in the Galactic Senate, Queen Amidala decided to take matters into her own hands by returning to Naboo to fight the Trade Federation and free her people. She was accompanied by her followers, the two Jedi, Anakin, and Jar Jar. While Jar Jar joined his fellow Gungans in creating a diversion to distract the [[Trade Federation Droid Army/Legends|Trade Federation Droid Army]], the Queen and everyone else infiltrated Theed to capture Trade Federation Viceroy [[Nute Gunray/Legends|Nute Gunray]] and force him to end the occupation of Naboo. In the [[Theed Hangar/Legends|hangar]], Qui-Gon and Obi-Wan encountered Darth Maul and dueled him while the others moved on. As the duel took them deep into the [[Plasma Refinery Complex|Theed Generator Complex]], Obi-Wan got separated from Qui-Gon and Maul after falling off a ramp. In the meantime, Amidala and Panaka fought their way through the streets of Theed, destroying Trade Federation battle droids and getting all the help they could from Naboo soldiers and civilians alike. After destroying a large [[force-field generator]] blocking their way, they arrived outside the [[Theed Royal Palace/Legends|Theed Royal Palace]] and broke in through a side window. ===The Final Battle=== Inside the Royal Palace, Amidala and Panaka had to find three [[Keycard/Legends|security pass keys]] that would grant them access to the Throne Room, where Nute Gunray was. Along the way, they cleared out Trade Federation battle droids and freed captured Naboo soldiers. They managed to corner Gunray inside the Throne Room and took him prisoner, ending the Trade Federation's occupation of Naboo. Meanwhile, Obi-Wan navigated his way through the Theed Generator Complex while observing Qui-Gon and Maul from a distance. He destroyed battle droids along the way and used the Force to [[Force jump/Legends|jump]] from platform to platform in an attempt to catch up with his master. By the time he reached the generator core, he saw Qui-Gon being struck down by Maul. After passing through the [[Laser gate/Legends|laser gates]], Obi-Wan dueled Maul and eventually defeated him. With his dying breath, Qui-Gon made Obi-Wan promise to train Anakin, whom he believed was the [[Chosen One/Legends|Chosen One]]. Before the credits started rolling, the people of Naboo celebrated their liberation with a victory parade in the streets. ==Gameplay== Visually, the gameplay is similar to third-person "over the head" view games, but actions such as jumping, climbing, or pushing objects are very limited, relying mostly on interaction and dialogue options. The player controls [[Qui-Gon Jinn/Legends|Qui-Gon Jinn]], [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]], [[Padmé Amidala/Legends|Queen Amidala]], or [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Quarsh Panaka/Legends|Panaka]] in different levels. The game also offers a variety of weapons and slightly different expansion of the same movie storyline and is rather similar to the 2005 [[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith (video game)|''Star Wars Episode III: Revenge of the Sith'' video game]]. ==Release and reception== ''Star Wars: Episode I: The Phantom Menace'' received generally mixed to positive reviews, and was praised for its graphics, exploration, action and sound. However, the game was criticized for its simple gameplay controls and camera angle which provided a short field of view. The PC version holds an aggregate score of 63.35% and 56.42% for the PlayStation version at Game Rankings. The PC version has slightly better and more detailed graphics than the PlayStation version. ==Media== {{Mediacat|from=1|imagecat=Images from Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace (video game)|soundcat=Audio files from Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace (video game)}} ===Editions=== *UPC 023272315184; [[May 18]], [[1999]]; [[LucasArts]] Entertainment Company; US PC edition[[Star *[[Water/Legends|Water]] }} ==Alternative options {{SectionFlag|1}}== {{Quote|Try me!|Obi-Wan Kenobi, before killing a room of Gungan guards|}} In almost every level, the player can commit "[[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]]" or "evil" actions without any negative consequences or repercussions such as having to restart the level or gaining dark side points. The options are: *Obi-Wan can destroy [[TC-14/Legends|TC-14]], along with other harmless [[Droid/Legends|droids]] and unarmed personnel, on the Trade Federation Ship. *Obi-Wan can kill all the Gungans in Otoh Gunga except Jar Jar, [[Rugor Nass/Legends|Boss Nass]], and the [[Gungan High Council/Legends|Gungan High Council]]. *Obi-Wan can kill Naboo soldiers and civilian men, women, and children in Theed. *Qui-Gon can kill almost everyone in Mos Espa (including [[Sebulba/Legends|Sebulba]] and [[Shmi Skywalker Lars/Legends|Shmi Skywalker]]) but not Anakin, Watto, Jar Jar, or Padmé. **However, doing so does have consequences in Mos Espa, as Anakin declares he will not help a murderer. This does not occur in Mos Espa Arena or Encounter in the Desert. *Qui-Gon can briefly flirt with [[Ann Gella/Legends|Ann Gella]] and [[Tann Gella]] in Mos Espa. *Qui-Gon can kill almost everyone in Jabba's court except Jabba himself. If he keeps using Force push on Jabba, he can destroy the interpreter droid without killing Jabba. *Captain Panaka can shoot anyone (except Queen Amidala) and anything he wishes on Coruscant, taking their items instead of trading for them. He can also destroy the protocol droid at the entrance to the Coruscant City Tours to avoid paying credits. *Queen Amidala can kill Naboo soldiers (except Captain Panaka) and civilians. She can also kill Nute Gunray instead of capturing him. ==Continuity== *Most NPCs in the game will either hold a dialogue with the PC or refuse to. [[Mat Rags/Legends|Mat Rags]] is the only NPC who is programmed to make a random decision on whether or not he will. *Due to a game bug, Qui-Gon can kill Maul on Tatooine. Interestingly, this will result in the player failing the mission, although on rare occasions this may not happen, and if Qui-Gon returns to Mos Espa's entrance, the guards will attack him for killing someone. *The only characters voiced by the same actors as in the films are [[Watto/Legends|Watto]], [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]], and [[Jar Jar Binks/Legends|Jar Jar Binks]]. *For reasons unexplained, [[Skywalker Ranch]] insisted [[Gamorrean/Legends|Gamorrean]] guards on Tatooine be cut from the game. They were replaced with an as-yet unnamed gray-skinned species, to which the [[Scavenger (Mos Espa)|Scavenger]] belongs. *Some NPCs were designed to look like Gillian Anderson, David Duchovny, David Bowie, and Sean Connery. Gillian Anderson and David Bowie inspired the appearance of tourists on Coruscant. Sean Connery inspired the appearance of a Naboo prisoner in Theed. David Duchovny inspired the appearance of a hobo in the Restricted Area of Coruscant. *Several events and quotes of the game mimic the [[original trilogy]], particularly in the Tatooine levels. For example, several thugs in Mos Espa claim that they have a death sentence in five systems, much like [[Cornelius Evazan/Legends|Cornelius Evazan]]. A Jawa sandcrawler can be found in Mos Espa Arena, its inhabitants killed by Tusken Raiders. The bar in the Mos Espa Arena is similar to [[Chalmun's Spaceport Cantina/Legends|Chalmun's Spaceport Cantina]], and a character almost identical to [[Max Rebo/Legends|Max Rebo]] is playing in the bar. The track "Attack of the Sandpeople" plays when attacked by Sand People. Also on Naboo when you have to push a stump into a pit, the track "The Magic Tree" plays when in the swamp mirroring Dagobah. *Inside the Mos Espa Cantina, the Menu Music of "X-Wing Alliance" can be heard. *For the level "Encounter in the Desert" no new voice lines were recorded: everything said by Qui-Gon, Watto or Shmi has been recycled from other parts of the game. This may be because the level was created quite late into production.{{YouTube|video=tlzsB4JpLlI|text=How The Phantom Menace Game Became a Sandbox RPG | Developer Interview Part 1 | FLANDREW|channel=@Flandrew|name=Flandrew}} *The game had cut features and content that were not finished or were removed for various reasons: **It was planned for the game to have a PvP component that would have pitted players against each other in an arena setting. This was cut because of time constraints, although a test arena was created.{{YouTube|video=rX9ECU1BvVw|text=How the Phantom Menace Game Almost Had Multiplayer | Developer Interview Part 2 | FLANDREW|channel=@Flandrew|name=Flandrew}} **A dismemberment system was initially planned but implemented that would allow Obi-Wan and Qui-Gon to cut an enemy's limbs off. The game would calculate the limb to be removed based on the position of the lightsaber strike when the enemy was killed. It was removed at LucasArts' insistence as it affected every killable entity in-game, including the slave children in Mos Eisley. **[[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]] was at one point meant to be a playable character. **Driveable vehicles were planned. Players originally were to be able to drive an [[Armored Assault Tank/Legends|AAT-1]] and a [[Single Trooper Aerial Platform/Legends|STAP-1]] in the game. Voice lines from Qui-Gon instructing Obi-Wan how to pilot them are still present in the game's audio files. **A side quest involving [[Klatooine paddy frog/Legends|Klatooine paddy frogs]] was cut from the final game, as were the related characters [[Jabba's porter]] and [[Jira/Legends|Jira]]. **An entire planned level of the game driving the [[Tribubble bongo/Legends|tribubble bongo]] in the [[Naboo Abyss/Legends|Planet Core]] also did not make the final cut; neither did a conceived Gungan battle level starring [[Roos Tarpals/Legends|Roos Tarpals]]. ==Sources== *{{SWArchive|url=collecting/news/lego/news19990426.html|text=New Products from Episode I}} *{{CollectorCite|6|Around the Galaxy}} *{{CollectorCite|7|Around the Galaxy}} *{{SWArchive|url=gaming/videogames/news/news19990915b.html|text=Episode I on PlayStation}} *{{CollectorCite|8|Around the Galaxy}} *{{InsiderCite|65|Red Five, I'm Going In: The History of Star Wars Video Games|Red Five, I'm Going In: The History of ''Star Wars'' Video Games}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} ==External links== *{{LucasArts|url=products/phantommenace|text=''Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace'' Official Site}} *{{LucasArts|url=static/pr/tpm_psx_press_release.htm|text=THE ''STAR WARS''® SAGA COMES ALIVE FOR PLAYSTATION SEPTEMBER 15 IN LUCASARTS' ''STAR WARS: EPISODE I THE PHANTOM MENACE''™}} *{{WP|Star Wars: Episode I – The Phantom Menace (video game)|Star Wars: Episode I – The Phantom Menace}} *{{WebCite|url=http://www.lucasforums.com/showthread.php?t=5630|text=TPM Q & A|work=LucasForums.com|archivedate=20160410095307|nolive=1}} *{{WebCite|url=http://www.replacementdocs.com/download.php?view.8258|text=Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace instruction manual|work=ReplacementDocs|archivedate=20101220041035}} *{{WebCite|url=https://store.playstation.com/en-us/concept/10004910|text=STAR WARS Episode I -
